fragility_points: A macro shock or crypto liquidation event before noon ET Apr 15.
A Binance-specific dislocation at the exact settlement minute.
Fast downside momentum if BTC loses the low-72k area before settlement.
key_assumptions: Binance BTCUSDT near 74.2k is a fair starting cushion into Apr 15 noon ET settlement.
No major macro or crypto-native shock hits before settlement.
Binance does not show unusual venue-specific distortion at the resolving minute.
main_logical_chain: The contract resolves only to the Binance BTCUSDT 12:00 ET one-minute candle close on Apr 15.
Current Binance spot was about 74.2k, giving a cushion of roughly 4.2k over the threshold.
That makes Yes the base case, but not as close to certainty as a 94.5% price implies because short-horizon crypto volatility can still bridge that gap.
main_thesis: The market's 94.5% Yes price is broadly justified by Binance BTCUSDT trading around 74.2k, but it slightly understates short-horizon one-minute settlement tail risk.
quote_anchors: Polymarket rule: final Close price higher than 70000 on the Binance 12:00 ET 1m candle
source_quality_view: Primary evidence quality is high because Binance is the governing venue; contextual independence is medium after adding Coinbase and CoinGecko checks.
strongest_disconfirmers: This is a narrow one-minute crypto threshold contract, so a 5%+ drop before settlement is plausible.
Binance-only settlement leaves some exchange-specific microstructure or operational risk.
strongest_supports: Binance ticker showed BTCUSDT at 74197.36, materially above 70000.
Recent Binance 1-minute candles in the verification pass all closed above 74000.
Coinbase and CoinGecko context prices were roughly consistent with Binance.
timing_relevance: This is highly timing-sensitive because the contract settles on one exact Apr 15 noon ET one-minute close rather than a broader daily average or end-of-day mark.
unresolved_ambiguities: Exact settlement-minute display conventions on Binance should be respected at resolution time.
Current snapshot evidence cannot rule out a rapid late selloff.
what_would_change_view: I would cut the probability materially if BTC breaks lower into settlement, a macro/crypto shock emerges, or Binance trades unusually weak versus other venues; I would move up if BTC stays stable well above 72k-73k into Apr 15.
